caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The source is a vendor-sponsored, AI-adopter survey rather than a representative US portrait-studio time-and-motion study.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Generative substitution of the purchased portrait is treated in terminal demand and capture, not as labor replacement.
inputs.pi_dist: Based on a vendor survey of AI adopters, not observed studio margin retention.
inputs.pi_moat: The record has no US revenue split between contracted volume work and retail/founder-led work.
inputs.t50_years: This is workflow adoption, not replacement of physical portrait capture.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No representative US portrait-studio adoption census was found in the reopened sources.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Do not treat this estimate as evidence of a succession wave.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of documentation is not proof that no owner will retire.
inputs.active_consolidators: The acquisition is historical and its target is not a typical $1-10M EBITDA portrait studio.
inputs.buy_mult: The score requests EBITDA multiples but the available photography evidence is SDE-based marketing guidance, not closed $1-10M EBITDA transactions; this is an imperfect proxy.
inputs.exit_mult: The same SDE-versus-EBITDA mismatch applies; the Lifetouch transaction is a non-comparable national-volume benchmark.
inputs.pricing_structure: No code-wide revenue split is available in the reopened sources.
The registered Census 541921 URL currently resolves to the official NAICS search interface without exposing the cited definition in its static page text.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $121,356 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541921); class <5: 8,742 firms, avg revenue/firm $199,817 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $31,271 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 561 firms, avg revenue/firm $841,077 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $131,628 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 221 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,410,688 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $220,773 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 123 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,717,894 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $581,850 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 21 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,950,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,026,675 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 14 firms, avg revenue/firm $83,742,571 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $13,105,712 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 21

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI materially automates culling, first-pass editing, retouching and administrative workflow, but not the physical shoot, client direction or final taste review.

l.detail: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) direct: labor compensation (L02) $1,850.6M / sectoral output (T30) $9,021.2M, year 2022

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. Reopened evidence supports workflow automation, weak retention/transferability, thin consolidation economics, and a pressured but not wholly melting demand profile. The kill thesis remains useful, with all authored limitations preserved and one additional non-fatal caveat about the exact Census URL's current static rendering.

sources.title: Census NAICS 541921 definition
sources.what_it_supports: The exact NAICS scope: portrait studios providing still, video, or digital portrait services, including school, passport, home and special-event photography.

sources.title: Aftershoot AI Workflows in Photography: 2025 Industry Report
sources.what_it_supports: A vendor survey of more than 1,000 AI-adopting photographers describes automated post-production, faster delivery, 60% emphasizing premium experiences and 70% reporting more price sensitivity.

sources.title: PPA headshot photography research
sources.what_it_supports: PPA's 2025 survey of 443 recent higher-income photography buyers reports ongoing headshot demand and average budget/spend figures for that selected consumer segment.

sources.title: How to Sell Your Photography Business
sources.what_it_supports: A specialist M&A advisory page states a 1.5-3.0x SDE range for photography businesses and identifies owner dependence, client relationships and transferable systems as valuation drivers.

sources.title: Shutterfly agreement to acquire Lifetouch
sources.what_it_supports: Shutterfly announced its $825m cash acquisition of Lifetouch, described as the national leader in school photography, evidencing a distinct consolidated volume-photography segment.

title: Photography Studios, Portrait
